exemplar.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #4: Do the ever-improving tools of an area of knowledge always result in improved knowledge? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Consistent focus on the prescribed title, using History and Psychology as focal AOKs to ground the discussion
Insightful critique of socio-political influences on the DSM-5, demonstrating awareness of how external factors shape knowledge production
Clear recognition of the value of authenticity in digitised historical sources, showing engagement with TOK concepts of credibility
Use of multiple scholarly citations indicates engagement with relevant literature and provides a foundation for the analysis
weaknesses: Arguments and examples are often descriptive rather than analytical, limiting the depth of TOK evaluation
Counterclaims are noted but not fully developed or weighed against the main claims
Key TOK terminology (e.g., ways of knowing, knowledge frameworks) is absent, reducing conceptual rigor
Structural coherence is weakâtransitions between AOKs and within paragraphs lack clear signposting
The conclusion fails to synthesize implications for the knowledge question or suggest further lines of inquiry

annotations.comment: âAutomatic Text Recognitionâ is introduced as a technological advancement but its impact on interpretation, sourcing and justification in historical research is not unpacked in TOK terms.
The critical examination of financial conflicts in DSM-5 demonstrates thoughtful engagement with socio-political influences on psychological classification systems.
A wealth of examples (VR, robotics, big data) is presented but the essay does not critically evaluate how each tool expands or constrains knowledge in psychology.
Insightful awareness that physical originals have an authenticity digital surrogates cannot replicate. This shows critical engagement with credibility and authenticity in historical knowledge.
Frequent long, complex sentences impede clarity. Vary sentence length and include concise signposting to guide the reader through your argument.
The conclusion restates earlier points but does not synthesize broader implications for the knowledge question or suggest avenues for further inquiry.
The inclusion of multiple scholarly citations demonstrates engagement with relevant literature, providing a solid foundation for analysis.

The introduction defines âtoolâ and âknowledgeâ but does not explicitly frame the central TOK knowledge question. Articulating the prescribed title as a clear KQ would strengthen focus and guide the subsequent analysis.

The thesis uses vague language (âmostly contribute,â âlimitationsâ) without defining criteria for âimprovedâ or âvalidâ knowledge. Specify what constitutes âimprovementâ to clarify the essayâs evaluative framework.
The claim that digital tools yield âmore accurate knowledgeâ is asserted without explaining what accuracy means in the context of historical methodology or how it is measured.
An example is mentioned at the end of this paragraph but the explanation is cut off, leaving the reader without a clear illustration of how digitisation concretely improves historical knowledge.
The digitalisation of the Womenâs Suffrage Banner Collection is a concrete example but remains descriptive. Connect it to TOK concepts like source criticism or corroboration to deepen the analysis.
The counterclaim on context loss is noted but lacks exploration of how historians might mitigate this risk, resulting in a superficial evaluation.
The reliance on external quotes is heavy, yet their credibility and relevance to the nature of knowledge production are not assessed, reducing analytical depth.
The shift into psychology is abrupt with minimal signposting or comparison to the history section, which weakens the structural coherence of the essay.
The discussion of eye-tracking asserts it eliminates bias but overlooks technological limitations and interpretive challenges in processing eye-movement data.
The fMRI example is descriptive rather than analytical; there is no discussion of how neuroimaging data shapes justification processes or potential methodological biases.
The counterclaim that VR reduces understanding of social interactions is asserted without evidence or exploration of how virtual environments can meaningfully model social dynamics.
The discussion of past pathologisation of homosexuality in the DSM highlights how classification tools embed cultural biases, enriching the evaluative depth.
Key TOK terms like âways of knowingâ or âknowledge frameworksâ are absent, reducing the conceptual rigour and depth of TOK engagement.
History and psychology sections remain siloed with minimal comparative analysis; integrating cross-AOK insights would deepen understanding of tool impacts.
The essay seldom explores the nature of justification or interaction between methodology and interpretation, missing a core TOK perspective.
Paragraphs lack clear topic sentences and signposting, making it difficult to follow how each point advances the central argument.
Technical jargon (e.g., âecologically validâ) is used without explanation for readers unfamiliar with specific methods, reducing accessibility.
The reference to a âfast-paced societyâ impacting tool improvement is overgeneralised and not connected back to cycles of knowledge production, diluting the TOK focus.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
